service.heroText: Cargo and liability insurance for every step of the logistics chain.
service.bodyParagraph1: Insurance programmes are tailored to cargo type and route, from basic cover to war and delay risks with top-tier underwriters.
service.bodyParagraph2: We guide policy issuance and claims, liaise with insurers and make sure compensation arrives without delays.

auto.heroText: We organize international road freight with full support at every stage. We handle turnkey projects, from selecting equipment and planning the route to delivery control and unloading. We provide insurance, process coordination, and transparent execution control.
auto.bodyParagraph1: Caravan Logistics organizes international road freight across Central Asia, China, the EAEU, and Europe. We provide end-to-end support for trucking projects, from route planning to unloading control, for both one-off shipments and regular business supply flows. We serve manufacturers, developers, distributors, and trading companies. For each shipment, we select the right equipment based on cargo type, tonnage, volume, and urgency, reducing logistics risks and keeping delivery schedules stable.
auto.bodyParagraph2: We cover the full road-freight cycle, from rate calculation and carrier selection to route control, delivery timing, insurance, and customs support. As a logistics operator with a dedicated manager and transparent pricing, we give businesses stable and manageable international logistics.

cases.title: Road Freight Cases
cases.subtitle: Four projects covering synchronized international supply, high-value cargo security, winter chemical logistics, and regular reefer deliveries.

case1.title: Case 1. Restaurant equipment supply from Europe and China to Russia
case1.route: 4 trucks from Europe, 3 trucks from China, total weight above 100 tons
case1.task: Organize an international supply of restaurant equipment for a premium facility, consolidating cargo from Europe and China with onward delivery to Russia.
case1.challenges:
• The project required around two months of preparation and synchronization between two international origins.
  • The shipment included a large number of product positions and originally fell under more than 100 HS codes.
  • Two oversized units weighed about 30 tons each.
  • Separate low-bed trailers and crane unloading were required for the oversized cargo.
case1.solution:
• Built the logistics scheme and synchronized simultaneous dispatch of 4 trucks from Europe and 3 trucks from China.
  • Optimized cargo classification and reduced the complexity of HS-code handling.
  • Added 2 Russian low-bed trailers for oversized units and arranged loading and crane unloading.
  • We organized customs clearance in Bishkek, coordinated all related documentation processes, and ensured the onward shipment of the cargo to Russia..
case1.result: A complex international supply of equipment was completed with synchronized flows, oversized-cargo handling, and onward delivery to the final destination within the agreed schedule.

case2.title: Case 2. High-value cargo delivery from China to Moscow
case2.route: China -> Moscow, 3-truck batches, total cargo value above USD 15 million
case2.task: Organize transportation of high-value IT cargo with lithium batteries from China to Moscow with elevated requirements for security, insurance, and control.
case2.challenges:
• Each loaded truck carried cargo worth approximately USD 500,000 to 800,000.
  • Total cargo value exceeded USD 15 million.
  • The project required lengthy preparation of contractual, insurance, and customs arrangements.
  • Security requirements were elevated throughout the route.
case2.solution:
• Negotiation and approval with the client took around three months.
  • The transportation was organized in stages: supplies were consolidated into around 10 lots of 3-4 trucks each, within a project totaling about 30 trucks.
  • Armed escort was arranged on every stage across Kazakhstan.
  • We completed full cargo insurance, customs clearance, and route-wide control.
case2.result: The high-value shipment was delivered in stages, on schedule, and in compliance with security, insurance, and customs requirements.

case3.title: Case 3. Chemical cargo in a tank container from Germany to Bishkek
case3.route: Germany -> Almaty -> Bishkek, 26 tons, winter season
case3.task: Organize international delivery of chemical cargo in a tank container during winter at a temperature of -10 degrees and resolve technical and customs issues along the route.
case3.challenges:
• The cargo froze in transit and had to be restored before unloading.
  • The required trailer for handling the tank container was unavailable at destination.
  • A rare trailer had to be found urgently and the cargo had to be reheated.
  • At the Almaty-Bishkek border, customs declaration documents from Kazakhstan were additionally requested and document-related issues arose.
case3.solution:
• Arranged the international movement of the chemical cargo and issued insurance coverage.
  • Quickly sourced the required trailer for further handling of the container.
  • Organized two days of cargo heating up to approximately 25 C.
  • Engaged customs specialists and resolved additional document requirements at the border.
case3.result: Despite winter conditions, technical complications, and additional document requirements, the shipment was supported through all critical operational and customs issues without losing control of the project.

case4.title: Case 4. Regular deliveries of metal products from Yekaterinburg and Saratov to Bishkek
case4.route: Yekaterinburg / Saratov -> Bishkek, around 100 trucks per month over 5 years
case4.task: Ensure stable regular delivery of metal products from Yekaterinburg and Saratov to Bishkek with control over timing, documents, and security across the entire route.
case4.metrics: Rebar and metal productsStandard euro trucks90-105 m3Up to 22 tons per truckAround 100 trucks per month5 years of regular operation
case4.solution:
• Built a stable scheme of regular deliveries and document checks before dispatch.
  • Verified compliance of the cargo with accompanying documents and arranged liability insurance.
  • Received daily updates on truck locations to control safety and delivery timing.
  • When fraudsters attempted to reroute the trucks, we promptly checked the number, restored contact with the drivers, and prevented deviation from the agreed route.
case4.result: For several years, we have been ensuring stable deliveries of metal products from Russia to Bishkek with a transit time of 4-5 days and regular control of route, documents, and security at every stage.

rail.heroText: We organize rail freight across the CIS, China, and Europe and work with both owned and leased rolling stock.
rail.bodyParagraph1: Caravan Logistics organizes international rail freight across Central Asia, China, the EAEU, and Europe. We provide full support for rail shipments, from route and rate calculation to station-by-station control and arrival at the destination terminal, handling both spot shipments and regular supply programs. We serve manufacturers, agribusiness, metallurgy, construction companies, and major distributors. Depending on cargo profile, weight, volume, and lead-time requirements, we select the optimal format: boxcars, gondolas, flatcars, or containers.
rail.bodyParagraph2: We cover the full rail logistics cycle, from rate calculation and rolling-stock booking to movement control, shipping documents, and unloading coordination. With a dedicated manager and transparent pricing, we provide predictable and cost-efficient rail logistics for business.

cases.title: Rail Freight Cases
cases.subtitle: Three projects where coordination, control, and operational discipline were critical to the result.

case1.title: Case 1. Rail transport of oversized special equipment
case1.task: Arrange rail transport for oversized special equipment in full compliance with applicable requirements.
case1.challenges:
• The first step was to determine the degree of oversize.
  • Obtaining the loading scheme took significant time.
  • Railway services were not aligned and requirements changed between stages.
  • The route and movement conditions required separate approval.
case1.solution:
• We organized the collection and approval of the loading scheme.
  • We supervised loading and securing in line with rail standards.
  • We coordinated stations and the relevant railway services.
  • We provided full support throughout every stage of the movement.
case1.result: The cargo was delivered successfully without violations or delays.

case2.title: Case 2. Rail transport of commercial cargo with border risk
case2.route: Rail movement of ceramic tile through border stations
case2.task: Move the cargo by rail through border stations without a return or stoppage risk.
case2.challenges:
• The station recorded an overload at the border.
  • There was a discrepancy between weighing results.
  • The cargo faced a real risk of delay and return.
case2.solution:
• We organized a repeat cargo inspection.
  • We negotiated directly with station representatives.
  • We supported on-site resolution of the issue.
  • We ensured correct paperwork and a repeat control procedure.
case2.result: The cargo cleared the border successfully and was delivered to the consignee.

case3.title: Case 3. Turnkey rail freight for regular supply programs
case3.route: Stable rail deliveries across an international route network
case3.task: Build stable rail shipments for different cargo types without involving the client in operational work.
case3.solution:
• Provided and allocated wagons.
  • Organized wagon placement on the siding track.
  • Handled wagon inspection and acceptance.
  • Managed loading and securing.
  • Agreed the shipment plan.
  • Prepared all documentation.
  • Controlled movement to destination station.
  • Organized unloading and wagon return.
case3.result: The client receives a turnkey rail shipment without being drawn into complex operations or direct interaction with railway structures.

multimodal.heroText: We organize international multimodal freight by combining several transport modes within one logistics solution. We deliver turnkey projects, from selecting the optimal transport chain and planning transfer points to controlling every leg and final delivery. We coordinate all participants, provide insurance, and keep timing transparent.
multimodal.bodyParagraph1: Caravan Logistics organizes international multimodal shipments using road, rail, and sea transport. This format is used when a single mode cannot meet the client's timing, geography, budget, or cargo requirements. We build route architectures that balance transit time, cost, and operational reliability while keeping one accountable operator across the chain.
multimodal.bodyParagraph2: We provide end-to-end support, from transport-scheme design and mode selection to transfer control, documentation, and terminal coordination. This gives clients one operating center for the entire route and lowers the risk of disruption between legs.

cases.title: Multimodal Freight Cases
cases.subtitle: Three multimodal projects: steel supply for construction, delivery from Europe via Turkey, and cargo delivery from China to Bishkek under one control chain.

case1.title: Case 1. Steel products delivery from Atyrau to Bishkek
case1.route: Atyrau -> Lugovaya railway station -> transfer -> road delivery to Bishkek
case1.task: Organize delivery of steel products and steel items from Atyrau to Bishkek using a multimodal scheme with timing control across the full route.
case1.cargo: Steel products and steel items for a construction project in Bishkek.
case1.solution:
• Aligned cargo readiness dates and the dispatch schedule.
  • Checked documents before the shipment started.
  • Organized rail dispatch from Atyrau to Lugovaya railway station.
  • After arrival at the station, arranged transfer to road transport.
  • Maintained control of the cargo on every leg and coordinated final delivery to Bishkek.
case1.result: The cargo was delivered to Bishkek under the agreed multimodal scheme, and the client received one managed logistics chain instead of coordinating rail and road stages separately.

case2.title: Case 2. Cargo delivery from Europe to Bishkek via Turkey
case2.route: Europe -> port of loading -> sea to Turkey -> transfer -> Bishkek
case2.task: Organize cargo delivery from Europe to Bishkek using a multimodal chain with sea transport and onward delivery to Kyrgyzstan.
case2.challenges:
• Several transport stages had to be linked into one chain.
  • Precise coordination in port and during onward movement from Turkey was critical.
  • The client needed one point of control for the full shipment to Bishkek.
case2.solution:
• Accepted the cargo at the port of loading in Europe.
  • Organized the sea shipment to Turkey.
  • Aligned transfer operations and onward movement of the cargo.
  • Prepared documents for the next leg.
  • Arranged road delivery from Turkey to Bishkek and controlled the route until handover to the consignee.
case2.result: The cargo was delivered to Bishkek under the agreed multimodal scheme with coordination of every stage from the port of loading to the final destination.

case3.title: Case 3. Cargo delivery from China to Bishkek
case3.route: China -> consolidation warehouse -> rail leg -> border crossing -> Bishkek
case3.task: Organize cargo delivery from China to Bishkek with control of every stage, from pick-up at shippers to arrival in Kyrgyzstan.
case3.challenges:
• The cargo was collected from several shippers.
  • Packaging and readiness had to be checked before dispatch.
  • Stable timing and correct documents were critical for the client.
case3.solution:
• Organized cargo pick-up from shippers in China.
  • Completed consolidation at the warehouse.
  • Checked packaging and marking.
  • Prepared shipping documents.
  • Organized the rail shipment to the border crossing, then arranged delivery to Kyrgyzstan and controlled cargo movement up to arrival in Bishkek.
case3.result: The cargo was delivered to Bishkek under the agreed scheme with timing and document control at every stage of transportation.

china.heroTitle: Freight from China and East Asia
china.heroText: We organize international deliveries from China and East Asia to the CIS and EAEU. We manage turnkey projects, from supplier coordination and cargo consolidation to final delivery control. We coordinate all parties in the chain, provide insurance, and keep deadlines transparent.
china.bodyParagraph1: Caravan Logistics has built a dedicated lane for shipments from China and East Asia, covering road, rail, and multimodal solutions. We do not just arrange delivery, we manage the entire supply chain, taking into account cargo specifics, supplier requirements, production timelines, and the realities of transit routing.
china.bodyParagraph2: Our team handles the full cycle, from supplier negotiations and cargo pick-up across China to consolidation, export documentation, and movement control through several countries. We develop tailored logistics solutions and keep supply flows stable while reducing the risk of delays, downtime, and extra costs.

cases.title: China Lane Cases
cases.subtitle: Four projects covering equipment consolidation, merchant export, urgent rerouting, and turnkey project logistics.

case1.title: Case 1. Equipment delivery for launching a garment factory
case1.route: More than 15 suppliers across different regions of China
case1.task: Organize the delivery of equipment from China for the launch of a garment factory within a tight deadline and without losses in completeness.
case1.challenges:
• The project involved more than 15 suppliers across different regions of China.
  • Cargo pick-up had to be completed from more than 15 separate addresses.
  • Each supplier had different readiness dates, packaging requirements, and shipment terms.
  • All equipment had to be consolidated into one coordinated shipment.
case1.solution:
• Organized staged cargo pick-up from all suppliers.
  • Checked completeness, packaging, and marking of the equipment.
  • Arranged temporary storage and consolidation.
  • Prepared the document package and monitored the cargo across every leg.
case1.result: The equipment was delivered in full and on schedule, which allowed the client to launch production on time. After the first project, similar shipments for this customer continued on a regular basis.

case2.title: Case 2. Supply from China to Russia under payment restrictions
case2.route: China -> Russia with buyout through an export company
case2.task: Secure delivery from China to Russia when the client could not pay Chinese suppliers directly.
case2.challenges:
• Direct payments to suppliers were constrained.
  • There was a real risk of procurement and shipment failure.
  • The client needed a legal and workable structure for purchase, export, and delivery.
case2.solution:
• Found manufacturers in China and agreed supply terms.
  • Checked the goods and organized buyout through an export company.
  • Prepared the document package, export formalities, and international delivery.
  • Handled customs clearance and the subsequent transfer of goods to the Russian client.
case2.result: The client received the goods on a turnkey basis with delivery to a warehouse in Russia. Payment, documentation, and compliance risks were removed, and the scheme is now used for regular supplies.

case3.title: Case 3. Urgent delivery from India to Kyrgyzstan via an alternative route
case3.task: Organize urgent delivery of industrial products from India to Kyrgyzstan under time pressure and with the standard route unavailable.
case3.challenges:
• The supplier had a limited export-license validity period.
  • The standard route via Iran became unavailable because of external restrictions.
  • An alternative logistics chain had to be built quickly.
case3.solution:
• Organized cargo pick-up from the shipper and container provision.
  • Handled export formalities in India.
  • Developed an alternative route via Mumbai, Singapore, and the port of Nansha.
  • Coordinated onward trucking into Kyrgyzstan.
case3.result: The project is in execution. The expected outcome is delivery through the alternative route within the agreed timeline while preserving the client's export window and supply continuity.

case4.title: Case 4. Delivery of a production line from China to Kyrgyzstan
case4.route: 44 trucks, 39 oversized moves, units up to 38 meters long
case4.task: Organize turnkey delivery of a full equipment package for a fired-brick plant directly to the construction site.
case4.scale: 44 trucks39 oversized movesLength up to 38 mDimensions above 6 m
case4.solution:
• Assessed logistics and customs risks and selected the correct supply model for Kyrgyzstan.
  • Built direct and transit routes via Kazakhstan.
  • Completed partial dismantling of the equipment and route engineering, including temporary infrastructure removal.
  • Supported customs procedures and organized a temporary customs control zone at the plant site.
  • Delivered the full production line as one project and additionally supported fixed-asset registration and VAT exemption matters.
case4.result: The project was completed on time. The equipment was delivered directly to the site, the client accepted the production line, and confirmed continued cooperation.

air.heroText: We ship urgent cargo on charter and scheduled flights with 24/7 support.
air.bodyParagraph1: The company has experience in organizing international air shipments within selected logistics projects. Air delivery was used in cases where timing was the key factor and cargo needed accelerated transportation.
air.bodyParagraph2: Within previously completed projects, we worked with international carriers including DHL and Turkish Airlines. The format and terms of air freight are defined individually depending on project requirements and cargo characteristics.

cases.title: Air Freight Cases
cases.subtitle: One project example with route, shipment profile, support scope, and a short solution summary.

case1.title: Case: Electronics / Urgent Production Supply

meta.cargo: Cargo type: electronic modules for a production line
meta.extra: Additional services: insurance, expedited customs clearance, terminal coordination

case1.desc1: The client, a manufacturing company, faced the risk of a production-line shutdown because of a component delay. Sea or rail delivery could not meet the required timeline.
case1.desc2: We calculated the airfreight rate, booked the flight quickly, and prepared the cargo in line with air-carriage requirements. After arrival, we arranged expedited terminal handling and coordinated road delivery to the warehouse. The shipment prevented production downtime and financial losses.

broker.heroText: Our in-house broker team clears cargo of any complexity and supports inspections.
broker.bodyTitle: Customs Clearance and Foreign Trade Support
broker.bodyParagraph1: We provide end-to-end customs clearance and foreign trade support for businesses, taking over every stage from preliminary calculation to cargo release. We work worldwide with a focus on the EAEU and the wider Eurasian region, ensuring speed, transparency, and full compliance with legal requirements.
broker.bodyParagraph2:
• Import and export customs declarations. Preparation and filing of customs declarations with support through final release.
  • HS code classification. Code determination in line with EAEU requirements while reducing the risk of customs value corrections.
  • Advance calculation of customs charges. Duties, VAT, and fees calculated before cargo arrival.
  • Full shipping-document package. Invoice, packing list, contract, specifications, and transport documents.
  • Permit and compliance documentation. We prepare the full set of supporting documents.
    • Declarations of conformity
    • Certificates of conformity
    • State registration certificates
    • Import and export licenses
    • Product notifications
    • Exemption letters
  • Customs procedures management. We select the correct regime for each shipment.
    • Import for domestic consumption (IM40)
    • Customs warehouse (IM70)
    • Transit
    • Re-export
  • Clearance at all customs posts in Kyrgyzstan. Bishkek, Osh, Naryn, and border crossing points.
  • Coordination with bonded warehouses and terminals. Placement, storage, inspection, and cargo release.
  • Financial cost consolidation. One electronic invoice covering all services and payments.
  • China procurement support. Supplier communication, document control, and delivery terms supervision.
  • Export VAT refund support in China and the Kyrgyz Republic. VAT refund procedures to reduce procurement costs.
  • Support for investment and project cargo. Equipment imports, production lines, and work under preferential regimes.
  • Automated code and charge selection. IT tools that reduce errors and speed up clearance.
  • Dedicated foreign trade manager. Control of timing, documents, and every supply-chain stage.

warehousing.heroText: Owned and partner warehouses help us consolidate, label, and prepare cargo for onward shipment.
warehousing.bodyParagraph1: We build warehouse infrastructure around the client's task through a proven network of partner warehouses in China, Russia, Kyrgyzstan, and the CIS. Depending on project requirements, we select the right warehousing setup by location, storage period, and cargo-handling conditions.
warehousing.bodyParagraph2:
• Partner warehouse geography.
    • European Union
    • CIS countries
    • China: Guangzhou, Yiwu, Urumqi
    • Turkey
    • Kazakhstan
    • Russia
    • Kyrgyzstan
    • Uzbekistan
    • Afghanistan
    • Iran
    • UAE
  • What we provide.
    • Selection of the right warehouse site for the task
    • Organization of temporary cargo storage
    • Consolidation and distribution of shipment batches
    • Coordination of loading and unloading operations
    • Control of cargo movement through the warehouse
    • Coordination with warehouse operators
  • Flexible capacity access. When needed, we quickly connect suitable warehouse capacity through our partner network, providing flexibility and speed in solving logistics tasks.

insurance.heroText: We insure cargo and liability at every stage of the logistics chain.
insurance.bodyParagraph1: As part of logistics support, Caravan Logistics offers insurance protection based on the transport format, cargo type, and delivery terms.
insurance.bodyParagraph2:
Format 1

Carrier Liability Insurance

Motor carrier liability coverage

This format covers liability arising during transportation. Depending on the policy terms, the coverage limit may reach the equivalent of up to USD 100,000.

Format 2

Cargo Insurance

Additional protection tailored to a specific shipment

Cargo insurance can be issued under different tariffs depending on cargo characteristics, route, declared value, and carriage terms. It covers the key risks related to damage, loss, and other unforeseen events in transit.
insurance.bodyParagraph3:
Insurance Support

We support the client on insurance issues throughout the shipment lifecycle, from selecting the right coverage format to preparing the necessary documents within the agreed workflow.

footer.description: We organize road, rail, sea, and multimodal freight, selecting the right logistics solution for each task. We work through a proven partner network so cargo arrives on the agreed schedule and in proper condition.
footer.copyright: Copyright ® Kaliev Salavat All rights Reserved

partners.title: Our partners
partners.text: We work with manufacturers, distributors, FMCG brands, and industrial companies across the CIS.

team.title: Our Team
team.subtitle: The people behind Caravan Logistics who deliver results every day.

history.title: History and Growth
history.subtitle: From a small team to a trusted logistics partner.

founded.title: Launch of the logistics business
founded.text: From 2008 to 2013, the company started operations in Bishkek as a logistics provider focused on international freight and cargo delivery across Kyrgyzstan and Central Asia. The core focus was transportation with its own fleet and forwarding services. During this stage, practical experience in international logistics was formed, partner warehouses were built, and a clear understanding of cargo-delivery processes was developed.

system.title: Creation of Gruzovik
system.text: From 2013 to 2017, Gruzovik was established as a company focused on road freight transportation. This direction became a key element in the development of transport logistics and formed the operational foundation of the group. Today, Gruzovik remains the largest division by headcount and shipment volume, ensuring stable road-logistics operations.

team.title: Formation of the logistics team 1.0
team.text: In 2017, the core team of logistics specialists was formed. Transportation lanes expanded to include rail logistics. The company joined AmCham and signed a contract with Kumtor Gold Company, strengthening its position as an international transport operator and logistics partner for large businesses.

expertise.title: Strengthening the organizational structure
expertise.text: In 2018, a separate management function for the road department was introduced, which allowed transport logistics to develop systematically. The company invested actively in employee training, built internal standards for logistics work, and improved both service quality and execution accuracy in international transportation.

infrastructure.title: Strategic transformation
infrastructure.text: In 2019, the company shifted from operating its own fleet to a forwarding model. This made it possible to scale logistics services and cover more delivery directions. The Caravan Logistics brand was registered, the company joined CILT, and business processes were rebuilt around a modern forwarding model.

digital.title: Digitalization and process resilience
digital.text: In 2020, a CRM system was implemented with support from the European Bank for Reconstruction and Development. The digitalization of transportation and client-management processes helped maintain uninterrupted cargo delivery during the pandemic and enabled social logistics projects, including the supply of medicines.

newDirections.title: A systematic logistics company
newDirections.text: In 2021, the company updated business processes, introduced an effective motivation system, and developed its corporate culture. It received licenses and confirmed qualifications through CILT. Joining the Chamber of Commerce and Industry of the Kyrgyz Republic strengthened the company’s position in the Kyrgyz logistics market.

fullCycle.title: Development of new business lines and talent base
fullCycle.text: In 2022, dual education in logistics was introduced in cooperation with GIZ and BFET, along with a three-level certification system for logisticians and dispatchers. After the Huawei project, a foundation was created for launching customs clearance and customs support services.

strategy2023.title: Shift to strategic management
strategy2023.text: In 2023, a five-year development strategy was formed, the organizational structure was redesigned, and a product approach in logistics was introduced. The company launched cargo delivery from China, strengthened multimodal transportation, and implemented a call center and digital tools for managing customer requests.

analytics2024.title: Analytics and process efficiency
analytics2024.text: In 2024, the company expanded analytics in logistics processes and continued preparations for launching the customs-clearance department. A balanced scorecard system and elements of end-to-end analytics were introduced, Caravan Cargo was launched for SMEs, and a new generation of specialists, logistics 2.0, was developed.

scale2025.title: Full logistics cycle for business
scale2025.text: In 2025, a customs procedures department was created and the company received customs-carrier status. Customs clearance and turnkey logistics services were expanded, an export company and office in China were opened, international directions were strengthened, and preparations began for entry into the Kazakhstan market.

modal.title: Leave a request
modal.description: Share your contacts and briefly describe the cargo — we will prepare a proposal and get back to you shortly.
modal.hint: By clicking send you agree to personal data processing.
modal.success: Thank you! We have received your request and will respond shortly.

forms.success: Thank you! Your request has been sent. We will contact you shortly.
forms.error: We could not send your request. Please try again later.

email.title: Free consultation
email.text: Get a freight quote and a proposed route for your cargo — by road, rail, or multimodal delivery.
email.subtitle: Talk to a specialist
email.description: Leave your contacts and our logistics manager will respond shortly.

widget.title: Always in touch
widget.text: We reply in messengers or by phone.

hero.title: We are a logistics company
hero.line2: An average of 10,000 shipments per year.

services.title: Our services
services.heroText: We provide professional road freight services for domestic and international routes. The team organizes reliable deliveries from Kyrgyzstan to Russia and imports from China to Kyrgyzstan using modern trucks with strict safety and timing control.

auto.bodyParagraph1: Caravan Logistics organizes international road freight across Central Asia, China, the EAEU, and Europe. We provide end-to-end support for trucking projects, from route planning to unloading control, for both one-off shipments and regular business supply flows. We serve manufacturers, developers, distributors, and trading companies. For each shipment, we select the right equipment based on cargo type, tonnage, volume, and urgency, reducing logistics risks and keeping delivery schedules stable.
auto.bodyParagraph2: We cover the full road-freight cycle, from rate calculation and carrier selection to route control, delivery timing, insurance, and customs support. As a logistics operator with a dedicated manager and transparent pricing, we give businesses stable and manageable international logistics.

services.gridSubtitle: Choose a card to open a dedicated page with a full description.

auto.title: Road freight
auto.text: Long-haul convoys across the CIS with 24/7 monitoring.

rail.title: Rail freight
rail.text: We reserve wagons, supervise border crossings and prepare paperwork.

multimodal.title: Multimodal
multimodal.text: Combine road, rail, and sea legs for complex projects.

broker.title: Customs brokerage
broker.text: Declarations, certificates and on-site support at customs.

china.title: China desk
china.text: Import/export flows from China with consolidation and insurance.

air.title: Air freight
air.text: Charters and scheduled flights with priority handling.

banner.title: Our approach
banner.text: For us, logistics is the precise organization of route, timing, and documents. We align shipment terms, select the right transport scheme, and supervise the cargo at key stages. This approach helps the client understand shipment status, plan the next steps, and make decisions based on real information.

stats.title: Caravan Logistics by the numbers
stats.item1: Shipments and deliveries completed on international routes
stats.item2: Supply projects managed by our logistics and brokerage teams
stats.item3: Clients continue to work with Caravan Logistics every year

upcoming.title: We are preparing smart tools for launch
upcoming.text: We automate customs calculations and HS code search so you get answers in seconds on the site.

card1.title: Calculator — customs assistant
card1.text: Select origin, cargo type and extras — the tool instantly estimates duties and gives you a ready checklist.
card1.small: We are testing formulas and adding AI tips.

card2.title: HS code finder
card2.text: Enter a product name or industry — the service suggests valid codes, requirements, and supply restrictions.
card2.small: We will add fast search across a catalog of 10,000 items.

faq.title: Popular questions
faq.text: This section brings together answers about our working format, shipment support, communication, and process control. If you need a consultation on your task, leave a request and a logistics specialist will contact you shortly.
faq.a1: For us, clear communication, up-to-date cargo information, and timely feedback matter. At any stage, the client should understand the shipment status and the key decisions made for the delivery.
faq.a2: We do not operate on short-term gain. Our model is long-term partnership.
faq.a3: Each project is assigned a personal manager who oversees the shipment from quotation to unloading, coordinates carriers, controls documents, and keeps communication transparent at every stage. Every shipment is calculated individually based on cargo, timing, geography, and budget.
faq.a4: The company has an internal control system including calculation checks, compliance with agreed terms, and review of non-standard situations. We systematically analyze errors to prevent repeated incidents.
faq.a5: We treat feedback openly and record every non-standard case for detailed review. Analysis of correspondence, actions, and processes helps us improve the system instead of ignoring the issue.
faq.a6: Our team has practical international transportation experience across China, Southeast Asia, Central Asia, the EAEU, and Europe. This helps us predict risks, account for transit and customs specifics, and handle complex cargo.
faq.a7: We insure shipments in accordance with contract terms. This reduces the client’s financial risks and provides additional protection in non-standard situations.
faq.a8: Our team regularly attends webinars and advanced training courses, and also develops internally through our in-house learning system. We also cooperate with educational institutions, building a talent pipeline and attracting future logistics specialists.

about.heroText: Our company was founded in 2008, and our founder personally worked in transportation as a carrier. Our goal is to create solutions that make logistics faster, more efficient, and more stable while ensuring reliable delivery and minimizing costs. We accelerate your business growth.

card1.title: Own fleet
card1.text: We allocate tilt, reefer or special trucks for each project, supervise drivers 24/7 and arrange permits.

card2.title: Rolling stock access
card2.text: We reserve wagons and platforms, prepare ETRAN documents and guide cargo through border crossings without idle time.

card3.title: Multimodal solutions
card3.text: We combine air deliveries with last-mile trucking, consolidate cargo and insure every leg.

about.valuesText: We work based on responsibility, transparency, and partnership, making decisions that are sustainable for the client, the company, and the market. Our approach is built on system thinking, professionalism, and continuous team development.

contacts.heroText: We respond to international logistics requests covering road freight, rail, and multimodal projects across the CIS and global routes.
contacts.formText: Send a request — we will prepare a quote and answer all questions about documents, timing and tariffs.

visit.title: How to find us
visit.subtitle: Three Caravan Logistics offices: Bishkek, Almaty, and Guangzhou. Each office has its own address, contacts, and map.

bishkek.title: Bishkek, Kyrgyzstan
bishkek.schedule: The office is open Monday to Friday from 8:00 AM to 7:00 PM. Days off: Saturday and Sunday.

almaty.title: Almaty, Kazakhstan
almaty.schedule: The office is open Monday to Friday from 8:00 AM to 6:00 PM.

guangzhou.title: Guangzhou, China
guangzhou.schedule: The office is open Monday to Friday from 8:00 AM to 6:00 PM.

portfolio.heroText: Photos from Caravan Logistics projects, team, partners, and day-to-day operations.
portfolio.empty: No posts in this section yet.

feature1.title: Caravan Logistics wished partners a Happy New Year
feature1.text1: Each December we wrap up results and prepare personal gifts for clients. In 2024 the team created branded holiday boxes and delivered them to every key office.
feature1.text2: The greetings include thank-you letters and an invitation to a private partners’ club to discuss plans for next year.

feature2.title: Caravan Logistics congratulated partners on the New Year
feature2.text1: The team prepared videos and postcards highlighting 2025 achievements and wishes. Holiday surprises include coupons for extra services.
feature2.text2: We thank clients for their trust and promise even greater engagement in joint projects.

block1.title: Infrastructure focus in the CIS
block1.text: We cooperate with major terminals and border transitions, reserve capacity and move cargo without delays even in peak season.

block2.title: Global routes
block2.text: We ship from China, Turkey, Europe and the USA with consolidation, customs brokerage and insurance on every leg.
